title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: In the natural world, survival often depends on the (0) ability to remain hidden or to appear as something else entirely. Animal mimicry is a fascinating biological strategy that (1) in many different forms across the animal kingdom. Some harmless species have evolved to (2) the appearance of dangerous predators to ward off potential attackers. This type of deception is particularly (3) among insects, such as hoverflies that look like wasps. Other animals use mimicry to blend (4) into their surroundings, making them nearly invisible to both prey and predators alike. This is known as camouflage, and it requires a high degree of (5) to environmental cues. Scientists are still (6) the genetic mechanisms that allow these creatures to change their color or shape so effectively. The study of mimicry not only (7) light on the power of natural selection but also provides inspiration for new technologies in fields like materials science and robotics. By understanding how animals manipulate light and texture, we can (8) innovative solutions for human problems. This ongoing research reminds us that nature has already perfected many of the most complex engineering challenges through millions of years of evolutionary trial and error in the wild.

example.explanation: “Ability” is the most general and appropriate term for a biological capacity.

questions.explanation: “Manifests” is a formal verb used to describe how a quality or trait becomes visible.
“Mimic” is the specific biological term for one species imitating the appearance of another.
“Widespread” is the correct adjective for something that exists over a large area or among many groups.
“Seamlessly” means smoothly and continuously, without any apparent gaps or joins.
“Sensitivity to cues” is the standard way to describe a biological response to the environment.
“Unraveling a mystery/mechanism” is a common metaphorical collocation for solving something complex.
“Sheds light on” is a fixed idiomatic expression meaning to provide new information.
“Develop solutions” is a standard collocation in engineering and scientific contexts.
